writeup: The day after Buffalo's mandatory minicamp wrapped, June 13 brought a flurry of post-camp analysis and one unavoidable storyline: Stefon Diggs. Head coach Joe Brady went on the 'Shout!' podcast and, while praising Diggs, made clear he has full confidence in the current wide receiver room — effectively pumping the brakes on any reunion buzz that had been fueled by cryptic social media posts between Diggs and Bills players. On the injury front, cornerback Dorian Strong's neck issue continued to cast a shadow, with the 2025 sixth-round pick reverting to the reserve/non-football injury list and facing a possible return no earlier than 2027. Meanwhile, feature-length profiles emerged on rookie WR Skyler Bell — a Bronx-raised hockey devotee turned record-setting UConn pass-catcher — while kicker Tyler Bass's continued absence from on-field kicking drills kept questions about Buffalo's special teams alive heading into training camp.
